data.title: Kim Yong-Bum, former CEO of Hashed Research, appointed as Policy Director of the South Korean Presidential Office
data.description:
Odaily Planet Daily News On June 7, South Korea's new President Lee Jae-myung appointed Kim Yong-beom, former CEO of Hashed Research, as the head of the presidential policy office. This position is at the ministerial level and is responsible for coordinating the direction of national policies. Kim Yong-beom was formerly the Deputy Minister of Economy and Finance of the Moon Jae-in government and has extensive experience in macroeconomics and regulation.
According to Hashed CEO Simon Kim, this move shows that the Lee Jae-myung government is friendly to cryptocurrencies and plans to promote stablecoins denominated in Korean won to prevent the outflow of national wealth. Simon also revealed that South Korean regulators are closely watching the trend of crypto policy in the United States and intend to learn from its regulatory path. (CoinDesk)

data.title: South Korea Appoints Kim Yong-beom as Presidential Policy Chief
data.description:
According to Odaily, on June 7, South Korea's newly elected President Lee Jae-myung appointed Kim Yong-beom, former CEO of Hashed Research, as the Chief of the Presidential Policy Office. This ministerial-level position is responsible for coordinating national policy directions. Kim Yong-beom previously served as the Vice Minister of Economy and Finance under the Moon Jae-in administration and brings extensive experience in macroeconomics and regulation.

Simon Kim, CEO of Hashed, stated that this appointment indicates the Lee Jae-myung administration's favorable stance towards cryptocurrency. The government plans to promote a stablecoin pegged to the Korean won to prevent the outflow of national wealth. Simon also revealed that South Korean regulators are closely monitoring U.S. cryptocurrency policy developments and intend to adopt similar regulatory approaches.

data.title: Gemini submits IPO draft to US SEC
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News Cryptocurrency exchange Gemini has "confidentially submitted a draft registration statement" to the US SEC, which is related to the proposed Class A common stock IPO. The statement said: "The number of Class A common shares to be issued and the offering price range have not yet been determined. The initial public offering (IPO) is expected to take place after the US Securities and Exchange Commission completes the review process, and the specific timing depends on market and other conditions." Previously, Circle submitted an IPO application in April and began trading this week. Cryptocurrency exchange Kraken is also reportedly considering applying for an IPO. (The block)

data.title: Federal Reserve Suffers $1,060,000,000,000 in Unrealized Losses As Central Bank’s Rate Hikes Pressure Bonds
data.description:
The Federal Reserve is now facing a whopping $1. 06 trillion in unrealized losses on its balance sheet. The New York Federal Reserve Bank, which handles the Fed’s bond transactions, just disclosed the losses, linking them to the central bank’s tight monetary stance
source: https://dailyhodl.com/2025/06/05/federal-reserve-suffers-1060000000000-in-unrealized-losses-in-2024-due-to-higher-market-interest-rates/

data.title: Elon Musk’s X and Polymarket Deal Pushes Onchain Prediction Markets Into Global Social Feeds
data.description:

source: https://cryptonews.com/news/x-polymarket-live-crypto-odds/

data.title: Coinbase Adds PancakeSwap to Listing Roadmap As the BNB Chain DEX Surges to Record $173,000,000,000 in Monthly Volume
data.description:
Coinbase has added the native altcoin of the top decentralized exchange (DEX) to its listing roadmap. Coinbase’s customers may soon be able to trade PancakeSwap (CAKE), the native asset of PancakeSwap, a DEX originally built on the BNB Chain.
source: https://dailyhodl.com/2025/06/06/coinbase-adds-pancakeswap-to-listing-roadmap-as-the-bnb-chain-dex-surges-to-record-173000000000-in-monthly-volume/

data.title: Ethereum L1 Reclaims Stablecoin Market Share From L2s as Bot Transfers Rise 11%
data.description:
In 2025, bot-driven transfers on Ethereum mainnet (L1) accounted for 57% of stablecoin volume and 31% of stablecoin transactions in May. According to a report released by CEX. io on June 4, bots executed over 4. 84 million transfers—more than $480 billion in value—marking an 11% rise in bot activity year-to-date
source: https://www.thecoinrepublic.com/2025/06/06/ethereum-l1-reclaims-stablecoin-market-share-from-l2s-as-bot-transfers-rise-11/
